author: han0110
content: Will work on upgrading `zkevm-benchmark-workload` (and  `ere-guests`) to use zisk `0.18.0` today, will also try to add a raw stateless input fixture mode so we don't need to integrate the guest IO serialization yet. So with your `elf` and `vk` you will be able to run the benchmark suite with serialized input fixtures (using the `--bin-path` flag).

author: jsign
content:
Nice. I'm starting to work on being able to fill EEST benchmarks with the new `statelessInputBytes`/`statelessOutputBytes` fields that we added for regular EEST tests.

With that, we could use EEST fixtures directly to pick the `statelessInputBytes` to send directly to the guest programs -- that's kind of the holy grail to simplify a lot of our tooling (e.g. stop using Reth to generate the execution witness in EEST benchmarks inside `zkevm-benchmark-workload`).

The main reason why I've to work on this separately for EEST benchmarks given we already have this working for EEST tests, is that filling EEST benchmarks with the Python spec is very slow (not possible). Usually, EEST benchmark fixtures are filled with Geth in `execution-specs`. For EEST tests, Python spec is fast enough so we fill with that. 

So that means that I need to make some changes such that Geth t8n returns what's needed to fill EEST benchmarks. 

Things are a bit messy now since zkevm@v0.4.1 is targeting `bal-devnet-7` -- so I've to discover how ready Geth is on that front such that the execution witness it generates matches theoretically what the Python spec would do (in theory they both should match). But given `bal-devnet-7` is quite new, not sure yet that will be the case.

So a bit of juggling balls for me in the next days, but if we can get both EEST benchmarks to also come with `statelessInputBytes` and `statelessOutputBytes` as defined in our specs; then we might be able to remove a decent part of `zkevm-benchmark-workload` complexity.

Basically it could always read EEST fixtures directly, avoid the `witenss-generator-cli` stuff, and pipe directly the bytes into the guest programs both for EEST tests and for EEST benchmarks. (assuming guest programs we use comply with zkevm-eest releases, which is also our goal). (And I think indirectly will simplify some ere-guests parts since can stop caring a bit about input preparation -- maybe only use case that need it is proving mainnet blocks)

author: han0110
content: That'd simplify a lot on `ere-guests` side for sure! And we can move the `(new_payload_request, execution_witness) -> stateless_input_bytes` to `zkboost`. Eventually `ere-guests` host code would be like a subset of `zkevm-benchmark-workload` that does some basic execution testing on CI.
